description: Conservation laws for continuous vorticity fields (2D/3D Euler equations). [length-matched]

facts.context: The Q_f = Σᵢ<ⱼ ΓᵢΓⱼ f(rᵢⱼ) conservation law for point vortices extends to continuous 2D Euler vorticity ω(x). The continuous analog Q_f[ω] is:
facts.truth: Q_f[ω] = ∫∫ ω(x)ω(y) f(|x-y|) dx dy, approximately conserved
distractors: Q_f[ω] = ∫∫ ω(x)ω(y) f(|x-y|) dx dy, exactly conserved always
Q_f[ω] = ∫ ω(x)² f(|x|) dx, a single-point integral formulation
Q_f[ω] = ∫∫ |∇ω(x)| |∇ω(y)| f(|x-y|) dx dy, gradient-weighted

facts.context: For 2D Euler flow, when f(r) = -ln(r), the continuous Q_f gives:
facts.truth: kinetic energy of the flow (exact conservation law)
distractors: enstrophy of the flow (exact conservation law)
angular momentum of the flow (exact conservation)
palinstrophy of the flow (exact conservation)

facts.context: For 2D inviscid Euler vorticity ω, the quantity Q_{e^(-r)}[ω] = ∫∫ ω(x)ω(y) e^{-|x-y|} dx dy is:
facts.truth: approximately conserved with frac_var ~ 10⁻³ in numerical simulations
distractors: exactly conserved as a Casimir invariant of the 2D Euler equations
not conserved at all and grows exponentially with time in simulations
conserved only for axisymmetric vorticity distributions, not general

facts.context: For 2D Euler flow, Q_{√r}[ω] = ∫∫ ω(x)ω(y) √|x-y| dx dy is:
facts.truth: approximately conserved, part of the Q_f family for smooth f
distractors: exactly conserved, derivable from Noether's theorem directly
not conserved due to the non-analytic behavior of √r at zero
divergent for all smooth compactly supported vorticity fields

facts.context: For the Q_f conservation law, why is the circulation-weighted form Q_f = Σ ΓᵢΓⱼ f(rᵢⱼ) better than the unweighted Σ f(rᵢⱼ)?
facts.truth: circulation weighting gives 10000x better conservation (frac_var 10⁻⁵ vs 10⁻¹)
distractors: circulation weighting gives identical conservation (frac_var 10⁻⁵ both)
unweighted form gives 10000x better conservation (frac_var 10⁻⁵ vs 10⁻¹)
both forms are exactly conserved so the distinction is purely aesthetic

facts.context: For 3D Euler vorticity, vortex stretching affects Q_f conservation. Under stretching by factor s, Q_f scales as:
facts.truth: quadratic in s — Q_f grows as s² because ω scales linearly with s
distractors: constant in s — Q_f is exactly preserved because stretching conserves it
linear in s — Q_f grows as s because vorticity scales linearly with s
inversely quadratic — Q_f shrinks as 1/s² due to volume conservation

facts.context: In 3D Euler, the kinetic energy corresponds to Q_{1/r}. Under vortex stretching, this quantity is:
facts.truth: exactly conserved — energy conservation is not broken by stretching in inviscid flow
distractors: proportional to the stretching factor — energy grows linearly with stretch rate
not conserved in 3D — only the 2D form Q_{-ln(r)} is an exact invariant
only approximately conserved — stretching introduces O(ε²) energy dissipation

facts.context: The ratio R_f = Q_{e^(-r)} / Q_{1/r} for 3D vortex dynamics is:
facts.truth: more stable than either Q_f alone — stretching effects partially cancel in ratios
distractors: more sensitive to stretching — ratio amplifies individual Q_f fluctuations
exactly constant regardless of dynamics — a true topological invariant
undefined for most vortex configurations — denominator Q_{1/r} can vanish

facts.context: The Q_f conservation mechanism works because when ω(x)ω(y) is large (both points in vortex cores):
facts.truth: velocities u(x) ≈ u(y), so the advective derivative term nearly vanishes
distractors: velocities u(x) ≈ -u(y), so the advective terms exactly cancel pairwise
the kernel f(|x-y|) approaches zero, suppressing the integrand entirely
vorticity gradients ∇ω vanish, so the material derivative of ω is zero

facts.context: The 2D Euler enstrophy Z = ∫ ω² dx is:
facts.truth: exactly conserved — in addition to energy, Q_f, and infinitely many Casimirs
distractors: approximately conserved — bounded but oscillating with amplitude O(Re⁻¹)
not conserved in 2D — only conserved in the 3D axisymmetric reduction
only conserved when the flow is irrotational in the bulk of the domain

facts.context: For Navier-Stokes with viscosity ν, the Q_f quantities decay as:
facts.truth: dQ_f/dt ∝ -ν (linear in viscosity), returning to Euler conservation as ν → 0
distractors: dQ_f/dt ∝ -ν² (quadratic in viscosity), vanishing faster than linear as ν → 0
Q_f is exactly conserved even with viscosity — diffusion preserves the integral
dQ_f/dt ∝ -√ν (square root dependence), with anomalous scaling at small ν

facts.context: If Q_f bounds could control ||ω||_∞ growth in 3D, this would imply:
facts.truth: regularity of Navier-Stokes solutions — no finite-time blowup would occur
distractors: nothing about Navier-Stokes regularity — Q_f bounds are purely kinematic
guaranteed finite-time blowup — Q_f growth implies enstrophy divergence
only 2D regularity can be established — 3D stretching invalidates Q_f
